Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: Prepared by: Johan Pauwels Date: 22/11/2024 Cegeka reference: PRD.4.5.006 DV1.1 CONFIDENTIAL Service Description PRD.4.5.006 Managed Application Delivery Controller F5 Table of contents 1.1 Service Description 4 1.2 Service identification 4 1.3 Service summary 4 1.3.1 Infrastructure management 4 1.3.2 The Infrastructure 5 1.4 Service features 6 1.4.1 Standard Service features 6 1.4.1.1 Infrastructure knowledge 11 1.4.1.2 Technical setup 11 1.4.1.3 Technical management 12 1.4.1.4 Housekeeping 12 1.4.1.5 Monitoring 13 1.4.1.6 Security and Compliance 14 1.4.1.7 Backup and Restore 14 1.4.1.8 Incident and problem management 14 1.4.1.9 Customers responsibility 14 1.4.2 Optional Service features 14 1.4.3 Requests 15 1.4.4 Out of scope 16 1.5 Service characteristics 17 1.5.1 Obligatory 17 1.5.2 Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 17 1.5.3 Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 18 1.5.4 Conditions 18 1.5.5 Service delivery location 18 1.5.6 Service Reporting 19 1.5.7 Data classification 19 1.5.8 Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer 19 1.5.9 Miscellaneous 19 1.5.10 RACI Matrix 20 1.6 Adherence to Cross-Functional Services 22 2 Service Level Agreement 23 2.1 Service Window 23 2.1.1 Service Level 23 3 Service Billing 24 3.1 Standard Service Billing 24 3.2 Optional Service Billing 24 3.3 Change Request Billing 24 4 Document control 25 5 Document approval 26

Section.name: Service identification
Section.Content: Service Managed Application Delivery Controller F5 Unique ID PRD.4.5.006 Service Model Infrastructure management (IaaS)

Section.name: Infrastructure management
Section.Content: This Service is the management and administration of a Cegeka pre-defined F5 platform (the Infrastructure). It offers a hosted ‘application delivery controller’ solution including the deployment, management, maintenance, and monitoring. This comprehensive service includes the installation and configuration of application delivery appliances in a cluster mode and its software to ensure application high-availability, load balancing, web application firewalling and secure access. The service can provide following functionalities, depending on the Customers’ needs: The service is delivered in different sizes (Small, Medium, Large).

Section.name: The Infrastructure
Section.Content: This Service is the technical management of virtual F5 environments which are dedicated set up for one particular Customer. The F5 nodes are based on appliances running on a dedicated Cegeka virtualization environment. The cost is included in the pricing of this Service. The service manages the components of the platform infrastructure. These components are: The drawing below depicts a logical model of the scope of the service. Figure 1: Scope of the Service

Section.name: Standard Service features
Section.Content: The service includes following: 24/7/365 break/fix support, Redundantly deployed virtual appliance HA cluster(s) in Active/Standby (2 node cluster by default), licenses and active support contract, The basic configuration of the application delivery appliance to enforce security policies and access controls, Continuous monitoring and management of the application delivery appliance to ensure optimal availability and performance, Updates and patches are implemented to keep the software up to date within the vendor’s support matrix, Urgent Product Security Incident Response Team (PSIRT) notifications can result in additional updates and/or patches as per guidance of the vendor, The service includes logging and reporting features, providing clients with detailed information about network activity and security events upon request, The managed application delivery service offers some advanced features, configured using our template. Activation and/or customization of advanced features are done upon request in time and materials, Cegeka offers expertise and guidance in application delivery configuration and optimization to maximize security and performance. Firewall rules can be implemented, ensuring that only authorized traffic is allowed to pass through the network, Daily backups of configurations and settings are performed, ensuring quick recovery in case of system failures or disasters, Cegeka maintains relationships with leading application delivery vendors to leverage the latest technologies and tools for enhanced availability and protection. Features include: IMPORTANT REMARK: These features are included in the license but activation of features, which are not enabled in the basic configuration, as well as customization of features is not included in the service. These activities can be done in time & material. Sizing guidelines To correctly size an LTM, the expected customer requirements, e.g. throughput, should be multiplied by three (3) for peak load reasons before searching the table below. Small X000354 Medium X000355 Large X000356 Type LTM-200M LTM-1G LTM-3G vCPU 4x 4x 16x vRAM 8 GB 8 GB 32 GB vDisk 200 GB 200 GB 200 GB Throughput 200 Mbps 1 Gbps 3 Gbps Concurrent Sessions 100.000 100.000 400.000 L4 connections per second 4.000 4.000 40.000 L7 connections per seconds 6.000 6.000 60.000 SSL TPS (RSA) 1.500 1.500 3.000 SSL TPS (ECC) 2.000 2.000 10.000 Features include : IMPORTANT REMARK: These features are included in the license but activation of features, which are not enabled in the basic configuration, as well as customization of features is not included in the service. These activities can be done in time & material. Sizing guidelines To correctly size an AWF, the expected customer requirements, e.g. throughput, should be multiplied by three (3) for peak load reasons before searching the table below. Small X000357 Medium X000358 Large X000359 Type LTMAWF-200M LTMAWF-1G LTMAWF-3G vCPU 8x 8x 16x vRAM 16 GB 16 GB 32 GB vDisk 200 GB 200 GB 200 GB Throughput 200 Mbps 1 Gbps 3 Gbps Concurrent Sessions 100.000 100.000 400.000 L4 connections per second 4.000 4.000 40.000 L7 connections per seconds 6.000 6.000 60.000 SSL TPS (RSA) 1.500 1.500 3.000 SSL TPS (ECC) 2.000 2.000 10.000 Features include: IMPORTANT REMARK: These features are included in the license but activation of features, which are not enabled in the basic configuration, as well as customization of features is not included in the service. These activities can be done in time & material. Sizing guidelines To correctly size an AWF, the expected customer requirements, e.g. throughput, should be multiplied by three (3) before searching the table below. Small X000360 Medium X000361 Large X000362 Type APM-200M APM-1G APM-3G vCPU 8x 8x 16x vRAM 16 GB 16 GB 32 GB vDisk 200 GB 200 GB 200 GB Throughput 200 Mbps 1 Gbps 3 Gbps Concurrent Sessions 100.000 100.000 400.000 L4 connections per second 4.000 4.000 40.000 L7 connections per seconds 6.000 6.000 60.000 SSL TPS (RSA) 1.500 1.500 3.000 SSL TPS (ECC) 2.000 2.000 10.000 CCU 500 2500 5000 Access Sessions 10.000 10.000 20.000 Features: IMPORTANT REMARK: These features are included in the license but activation of features, which are not enabled in the basic configuration, as well as customization of features is not included in the service. These activities can be done in time & material. One Size Type DNS-250 vCPU 4x vRAM 8 GB vDisk 200 GB Throughput 1 Gbps DNS RPS 250.000 Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the infrastructure in order to perform the Service. Cegeka decides in the configuration of the Infrastructure in order to configure it up according to the technology requirements of the Infrastructure and in line with the best practices of the vendor. Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer. Every appliance is by default tuned according to Cegeka’s baseline of functional and security requirements. Some example settings: Appliance on a dedicated virtualization environment for such appliances Isolation and Segregation : Cegeka chose to implement a dedicated virtualization environment so that every appliance is isolated from other virtual machines and workloads. This segregation ensures that the performance and stability of these critical appliances are not impacted by the resource demands of other virtual machines. Customization and Configuration Flexibility : Running an appliance on a dedicated host allows for customizing the hypervisor configuration to meet the specific needs of these appliances. It enables us to fine-tune virtual machine settings and networking configurations for maximum efficiency and functionality. Security Enhancements: With a separate environment, it becomes easier to implement strict security measures and access controls. Access to the management interfaces of network and security appliances can be restricted to authorized personnel only, reducing the risk of unauthorized access or attacks. Resource Guarantee/ Performance Optimization : the Service is a critical component that requires predictable and guaranteed resources. A dedicated hypervisor environment allows us to allocate resources exclusively to these appliances, ensuring consistent performance. An appliance for this Service requires significant computing resources. Providing a dedicated virtualization environment allows these appliances to access the necessary CPU, memory, and storage (local) resources without contention from other virtual machines, leading to optimal performance. Scalability and Redundancy: Cegeka has chosen to use the high availability and redundancy configurations that are embedded in the Service itself without relying on hypervisor redundancy. Oversubscription : It is important to note that we don’t use any oversubscription in the usage of compute and storage resources. We only use the available capacity up to and around 80% of the physical limit of the host. Activity of a virtual machine therefore can’t cause any deterioration in the performance of another virtual machine. Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Infrastructure and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows. Managing the (security) log file generation (via syslog) and storing the log files of the device on a system of Cegeka for at least 6 months. The storage boundary is managed / guarded by a fair use policy. When an appliance becomes obsolete the instance and associated resources will be removed. When an instance is destroyed the data is permanently removed. This operation is irreversible! Any used license will be returned to the Cegeka license pool. Licenses are not transferable. Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Infrastructure. Automation is applied where possible. Proactive management Housekeeping includes following regular checks: Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA. Maintain best practice performance optimization techniques. Patching The service includes a maximum of 2 patch cycles (per environment) per year for the managed service. Patching is done in accordance to the operational Patch Plan. Note: critical patches are subject to immediate patching in accordance with the cross-functionals. Life Cycle Management Cegeka respects the operating system lifecycle as provided by the vendor. Cegeka monitors the Infrastructure and notifies the Customer timely when they are subject to end-of-life. This includes, if applicable, the extension or replacement of the Infrastructure. Cegeka also notifies the Customer upon newer versions of the Infrastructure in order to plan the update of the platform in time within the followed (relative) version (for example N-1). Life Cycle Updates will be planned in accordance with the agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis. The customer will be informed at least 6 months in advance to properly plan the upgrade, as significant customer effort is required in application testing. Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Infrastructure. Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service. Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management. In case the Customer uses co-management, additional monitoring can be set up with a Customer dedicated monitoring solution (e.g. Dynatrace). Management of technical users The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies. Detection of security incidents The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. Application logs for SIEM purposes or compliance purposes Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request. The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance to the Customer backup policy. Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored. Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals). Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals). The Customer can have administrative access to the environment(s) to be able to manage their configurations. The customer must register his change activities in the Cegeka ITSM system for audit reasons.

Section.name: Optional Service features
Section.Content: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. The following optional features are possible with the Service:

Section.name: Requests
Section.Content: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). request type Description Lead time Service Requests (no pre-approved service requests for this service) n/a Normal Changes Any other request. Examples: Extension or modifications of the Infrastructure Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.

Section.name: Out of scope
Section.Content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service. Operational changes NOT in scope The service does not cover operational changes on customer request. These changes will be charged in time and material. Example operational changes include but are not limited to:

Section.name: Obligatory
Section.Content: The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:

Section.name: Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Cross-Functional Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License included in service applies to Appropriate F5 licenses for the contracted services Licenses are included in the pricing of the service. Due to partner agreement between Cegeka and F5, licenses are yearly subscribed. Each license is always accompanied by valid maintenance contract throughout the lifecycle of the component.

Section.name: Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are not included in this Service. It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License description/remarks (none specific) Administration of licenses involves: License compliancy Customer has to share the available licenses with Cegeka. Cegeka will assist in customer license compliance by providing knowledge and reporting on use of licenses and license requirements. Cegeka ensures to implement or activate features that are only in-line with the licenses owned by the customer.

Section.name: Conditions
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: Service delivery location
Section.Content: This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.

Section.name: Service Reporting
Section.Content: Monthly reporting will be provided to inform on the availability and meeting the SLA’s and to provide insights on the capacity and expected extensions required.

Section.name: Data classification
Section.Content: Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service. Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service. In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).

Section.name: Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer
Section.Content: This service is delivered with: Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues): F5 Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party): (none)

Section.name: Miscellaneous
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: RACI Matrix
Section.Content: Activity Cegeka Customer Technical platform management A,R I Change management A,R C Executing standard requests A,R I Executing non-standard requests A,R I Architecture & Design Evaluate & Selection Platform (Hardware & Software) A,R Evaluate & Selection Platform ITSCM (IT Service Continuity Management) A,R Lifecycle Management Platform (Hardware & Sofware) A,R HA/DR/Backup requirements A,R HA/DR/Backup solution, installation and configuration A,R I License Management Platform Licensing model A,R Platform Licensing model Compliance A,R Plan & Deliver Platform IaaS, Network & Connectivity A,R Implement Platform Backup A,R Change Management A,R C Request Fullfilment A,R I Housekeeping (manage & assure) Trigger Platform Technology Refresh (Hardware & Software) (lifecycle management) A,R C Platform Operations (Hardware & Sofware) A,R C Firmware Patch Management A,R I Monitoring Platform Capacity A,R I Platform Monitoring (availability, performance, backup status) & Alerting A,R Operate & Maintain Platform Backup A,R Incident Management (proactive by monitoring) A,R I Problem Management (proactive by monitoring) A,R I Evaluate and assess patch and upgrade effect on infrastructure A,R C Functional testing after deploying patches and upgrades C A,R Security Platform Technology security A,R I Advise and follow up of security and auditing measures A,R I Security Patch Management A,R I Customer responsibility (in co-management) Virtual server (F5) configuration(*) C A,R Troubleshooting of application related issues C A,R (*)Argenta users will receive the ‘Manager’ role on F5-level. This role grants users permission to create, modify and delete virtual servers, pools, pool members, nodes, custom profiles, custom monitors, and iRules. These users can view all objects on the system. (*)Cegeka users will receive the ‘Administrator’ role on F5-level. This role grants complete access to all objects on the system. RACI role definition:

Section.name: Adherence to Cross-Functional Services
Section.Content: The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services n/a The Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Section.name: Service Window
Section.Content: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Section.name: Service Level
Section.Content: Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. Availability The following Availability KPI’s are provided for the Service: KPI ID KPI name Availability Service Window Metric KPI.4.5.006.AVAIL Availability 24/7 Monthly: 99,5% Yearly (sliding window): 99,9% KPI.4.5.006.MTBF Mean time between failures 24/7 6 months The availability measurement is monthly and calculated as:

Section.name: Standard Service Billing
Section.Content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. ID Component Billing element BILLING UNIT SKU.4.5.006.L01 LTM cluster LTM 2node cluster Small Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L02 LTM 2node cluster Medium Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L03 LTM 2node cluster Large Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L05 LTM 4node cluster Medium Per 4node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L11 AWF cluster AWF 2node cluster Small Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L12 AWF 2node cluster Medium Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L13 AWF 2node cluster Large Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L21 APM cluster APM 2node cluster Small Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L22 APM 2node cluster Medium Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L23 APM 2node cluster Large Per 2node cluster SKU.4.5.006.L31 DNS DNS 2node cluster (one size) Per 2node cluster Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a monthly basis: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Section.name: Optional Service Billing
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: Change Request Billing
Section.Content: Changes are billed on a monthly basis. Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact. Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.

Section.name: Document control
Section.Content: Document version Date Author Notes DV1.0 13/06/2024 Johan Pauwels First version aligned with structural Service Description requirements. DV1.1 22/11/2024 Johan Pauwels Comments removed, document cleanup for PDF.
